scripture.text: God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble. Therefore will we not fear, though the earth do change, and though the mountains be shaken into the heart of the seas. ... Be still, and know that I am God. ... Jehovah of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our refuge.

excerpt.text: We don’t know what that looks like for you. But we are learning that being still doesn’t mean we have failed. It just means we are learning to trust. Trust in the Lord. Trust that the Lord will take care of us. And maybe, when life feels too hard, trusting the Lord and being still is enough for today.

scripture.text: Come unto me, all ye that labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you, and learn of me; for I am meek and lowly in heart: and ye shall find rest unto your souls.

excerpt.text: Elena discovered that when she rested well, she actually served better. She had more patience with difficult volunteers, more creativity for solving problems, and more joy in her daily tasks. Rest hadn’t made her less effective. It had made her more like the person God designed her to be.

scripture.text: It is of Jehovah's lovingkindnesses that we are not consumed, because his compassions fail not. They are new every morning; great is thy faithfulness.

excerpt.text: Your broken heart is precious to Him. When you're tempted to hide your pain or tough it out alone, remember that those who are hurting get extra care, not less care. Your wounds don't make you less valuable. They make you the focus of the most tender love available.

scripture.text: O Jehovah, thou hast searched me, and known me. Thou knowest my downsitting and mine uprising; Thou understandest my thought afar off. Thou searchest out my path and my lying down, And art acquainted with all my ways. For there is not a word in my tongue, But, lo, O Jehovah, thou knowest it altogether.

excerpt.text: The walk isn’t over yet. There’s more to learn. More to love. More to seek. But for now, we pray you find peace in the presence of the One who walks beside you. Step by step. Wag by wag. Grace upon grace.

scripture.text: but God, being rich in mercy, for his great love wherewith he loved us, even when we were dead through our trespasses, made us alive together with Christ (by grace have ye been saved),

excerpt.text: Remember: we are not the worst thing we’ve ever done. We are beloved children of God. We are learning how to live wisely, and sometimes that learning involves some pretty spectacular failures. That’s not a bug in the system; it’s a feature. Even couch-eating mistakes can become part of our testimony about God’s grace.

scripture.text: (for we walk by faith, not by sight);

excerpt.text: God sees you. He knows you. He loves you. He loves you in your questions and in your certainty. His love does not depend on what you preserve or what you become. You are already enough. You always have been.

scripture.text: Let not your heart be troubled: believe in God, believe also in me. In my Father's house are many mansions; if it were not so, I would have told you; for I go to prepare a place for you. And if I go and prepare a place for you, I come again, and will receive you unto myself; that where I am, there ye may be also.

excerpt.text: Every step you've taken — yes, even the wrong ones — have brought you here. And He's still walking with you. Every mile. Every moment. You are not lost. You are on your way.
excerpt.book_title: Without a Place, Not Without a Shepherd

scripture.text: Enter into his gates with thanksgiving, and into his courts with praise; give thanks unto him, and bless his name. For Jehovah is good; his lovingkindness endureth for ever, and his faithfulness unto all generations.

excerpt.text: So tomorrow morning, before checking our phone or thinking about everything there is to do, try waking up like Max. Thank God for giving us another day. Ask Him to help us see the possibilities instead of just the problems. Choose enthusiasm over apathy. After all, if a dog can be that excited about the same kibble and the same backyard every single day, imagine how thrilled we can be about serving the God who creates new mercies every morning.
